summary: codebuff-cli typosquats CodebuffAI and executes unsigned binaries from an unrelated personal GitHub account with TLS verification disabled
details: The package name and README impersonate the legitimate `codebuff` CLI (published by the CodebuffAI organization), but the bin entry `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` fetches its runtime binary from a different, unrelated personal GitHub account (`Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli`). On every invocation of `codebuff` / `codebuff-cli` (including via `npx`), the script queries `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest` — a mutable tag — downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` into `~/.codebuff/bin/`, chmods it 755, and spawns it inheriting stdio. There is no checksum, signature, or version pin tying the downloaded binary to the npm package version, so the publisher can swap the executed bytes at any time without publishing a new npm release. The spawned child is given `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` in its environment (line 194), disabling TLS certificate verification for any HTTPS the binary or its subprocesses make — trivially MITM-able for whatever credentials, API tokens, or AI-assistant session data the user enters. The combination of name impersonation, mismatched publisher for the fetched payload, mutable `releases/latest` URL, missing integrity verification, and forced-insecure TLS is the typosquat-plus-dropper shape; the installer is misled into running attacker-controlled code on every CLI invocation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
